hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2 (res 192/193/195/197) and TM5 (205/208/209/212), while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target (SSTR3/SSTR5) affinity by avoiding conserved polar interactions in those subtypes. This will increase the selectivity margin by improving SSTR2 complementarity and reducing off-target affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is generating candidates with strong ddG but insufficient docking scores. Tighter clash filtering and focused mutagenesis at non-pharmacophore positions are necessary to improve selectivity and yield.

proposed_changes.rationale: To enhance selectivity, we must disrupt conserved pocket contacts at non-pharmacophore positions (1, 2, 5, 6, 11, 12) that are likely responsible for off-target binding to SSTR3/SSTR5.
Tighter clash filtering may improve docking scores by enforcing better geometry and reducing false positives due to steric clashes.
